name: Caddy
description: Automatic HTTPS reverse proxy. Routes clean URLs to all your services with zero cert management.

preflight_checks.description: Port 80 must be available for Caddy HTTP
Port 443 must be available for Caddy HTTPS

steps.instruction: Caddy is your reverse proxy — it auto-configures from the generated Caddyfile. No manual setup needed unless you're using a custom domain

If you entered a domain during setup: go to your DNS provider (Cloudflare, Namecheap, etc.) → DNS Records → Add an A record: Name = * (wildcard), Value = your server's PUBLIC IP address, TTL = Auto. This makes *.yourdomain.com point to your server

Caddy will automatically request and renew free Let's Encrypt TLS certificates. First request takes ~30 seconds. After that, access any service at https://appname.yourdomain.com (e.g. https://radarr.yourdomain.com)
Without a domain: services are available at http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:PORT directly. No HTTPS needed for local access
